preamble: The phonetic transformation from a voiced aspirated bilabial plosive (bʱ) to a voiceless aspirated bilabial plosive (pʰ) is a historically attested sound change primarily characterized by devoicing. This shift involves the loss of vocal fold vibration during the articulation of the stop, while the aspiration (a puff of air following the release) and the bilabial plosive articulation are maintained. This change is considered phonetically natural, as voiced aspirated stops are typologically less common and often undergo simplification.
phoneticEffects: Devoicing, Aspiration maintenance, Fortition (loss of voicing)

examples.note: Proto-Indo-European voiced aspirated stops, such as *bʰ, underwent devoicing to become voiceless aspirated stops (pʰ) in Ancient Greek.

sources: Reddit. (2022, May 31). Help explaining the reconstruction of PIE. r/linguistics.
de Melo, W. D. C. (n.d.). Faliscan Reflexes of the Indo-European voiced aspirates.
